data.title: Securitize appoints former IMF representative to its board of directors.
data.description: Securitize announced the appointment of investor, Blackstone advisor, and former IMF board member Sunil Sabharwal to its board of directors. Sunil Sabharwal served as the IMF's U.S. representative, confirmed by the U.S. Senate, from 2016 to 2018, for which he received the U.S. Treasury Department's Distinguished Service Award. He currently serves on the boards of Thunes and TookiTaki, and previously served as chairman of payments companies Earthport and Ogone, which were acquired by Visa and Ingenico, respectively. Since 2021, he has served as an advisor and operating partner of the Blackstone Growth Equity Fund. Securitize currently manages over $4 billion in on-chain assets, including the BlackRock BUIDL fund and tokenized products from institutions such as Apollo, BNY, Hamilton Lane, KKR, and VanEck. The company reached an acquisition agreement with a Cantor Fitzgerald subsidiary last October, valuing Securitize at $1 billion. The merged company will be listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol CEPT.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Portal Unveils Portal 2.0 with AI-Driven Game Development Focus
data.description:
Portal, a cross-chain gaming platform, has announced the launch of Portal 2.0. According to Foresight News, the update is led by a new leadership team supported by Animoca Brands, with former Ubisoft game director Benjamin Charbit appointed as CEO. Portal is shifting its strategic focus towards AI-native tools and creator-first workflows, aiming to enable game development driven by AI, allowing non-developer users to create content.

The tools introduced by Portal include Portal Studio, a virtual workspace for agent collaboration, and Portal GameRouter, a single API interface for managing agents, assets, and pipelines. Additionally, Portal plans to launch Portal GameGen, which supports prompt-generated games, and Portal GameHub, a platform for publishing and monetization. In terms of token economics, Portal intends to periodically buy back PORTAL tokens using revenue generated from its AI tools. PORTAL token holders will enjoy early access, premium tool usage, and voting rights.

Previously, Portal completed a merger with BLIFE Protocol in December 2025, and its core team is now primarily composed of representatives from Animoca Brands.

data.title: Securitize Appoints Sunil Sabharwal to Board
data.description:
Securitize has announced the appointment of Sunil Sabharwal, an investor and advisor to Blackstone, as well as a former board member of the International Monetary Fund (IMF), to its board of directors. According to Odaily, Sabharwal served as the U.S. representative to the IMF from 2016 to 2018, a position confirmed by the U.S. Senate, and was awarded the U.S. Treasury Department's Distinguished Service Award. He is currently a board member of Thunes and TookiTaki and has previously chaired payment companies Earthport and Ogone, which were acquired by Visa and Ingenico, respectively. Since 2021, he has been an advisor and operating partner at Blackstone Growth Equity Fund.

Securitize manages over $4 billion in on-chain assets, including tokenized products from institutions such as BlackRock, Apollo, BNY, Hamilton Lane, KKR, and VanEck. Last October, the company reached an acquisition agreement with a subsidiary of Cantor Fitzgerald, valuing Securitize at $1 billion. The merged entity is set to list on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol CEPT.

data.title: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff: Large-scale military operations against Iran can be resumed in a short period of time.
data.description: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff General Kaine stated that the US is prepared to resume large-scale military operations against Iran in a short period of time. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Coinbase will list Opengradient (OPG)
data.description: According to official sources, Coinbase will launch spot trading of Opengradient (OPG).

data.title: Iranian media: Iran has not sent any delegation to Pakistan to date.
data.description: According to the Tehran Times: Iran has so far not sent a delegation of any level to Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance Alpha will list USDAI (CHIP)
data.description: According to official sources, Binance Alpha will list USDAI (CHIP) on April 21st. Eligible users can claim the airdrop using Binance Alpha Points on the Alpha event page after trading opens on Alpha. Further details will be announced separately.

data.title: Binance Alpha to List USDAI (CHIP) on April 21
data.description:
Binance announced on X that Binance Alpha will become the first platform to list USDAI (CHIP) on April 21. Eligible users will have the opportunity to claim an airdrop using their Binance Alpha Points once trading opens on the Alpha platform. This initiative marks a significant development for users looking to engage with USDAI (CHIP) through Binance Alpha.

The airdrop will be accessible to users who meet the eligibility criteria, allowing them to utilize their accumulated Binance Alpha Points. This event provides an opportunity for users to participate actively in the platform's offerings. Further details regarding the airdrop and trading specifics will be announced separately. Users are encouraged to stay informed through Binance's official channels to ensure they do not miss out on any updates related to this listing.

data.title: US Stock Futures Rise Ahead of Iran Developments and Kevin Warsh's Senate Hearing
data.description: US stock-index futures experienced an uptick on Tuesday morning as traders anticipated key developments regarding Iran and the Senate hearing of Kevin Warsh. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the market's focus on these events amid the ongoing corporate earnings season. Investors are closely monitoring geopolitical tensions and potential policy shifts that could impact market dynamics.

data.title: Binance to List Chip (CHIP) with Seed Tag
data.description: Binance, a prominent centralized exchange (CEX), has announced its decision to list Chip (CHIP) with a seed tag. According to BWEnews, this move is part of Binance's ongoing efforts to expand its range of listed cryptocurrencies. The seed tag indicates that Chip is a relatively new and potentially high-risk asset, which investors should approach with caution. The listing is expected to attract attention from traders interested in exploring new digital assets. Binance continues to play a significant role in the cryptocurrency market by providing a platform for emerging tokens.

data.title: Attempted Armed Robbery of Crypto Professional in France
data.description: A 40-year-old cryptocurrency professional in Saint-Jean-de-Védas, near Montpellier, France, experienced an attempted armed robbery at his home. According to ChainCatcher, the suspect disguised as a delivery person and entered the residence, demanding the victim's crypto wallet private keys. The victim managed to overpower the suspect and seize the handgun, during which a gunshot was fired, but no injuries were reported. The suspect fled the scene but was apprehended by police after a three-day pursuit. Local courts have charged the individual with attempted armed robbery, and prosecutors suggest he may have been acting under the direction of an accomplice.

data.title: Strategy overtakes BlackRock's IBIT as the world's largest bitcoin holder for the first time since 2024
data.description: Key takeawaysStrategy now holds 815,061 BTC, surpassing BlackRock's IBIT at 802,824 BTC — a lead of more than 12,000 BTC.This marks Strategy's first time ahead of IBIT since Q2 2024, when BlackRock's ETF overtook it with roughly 273,000 BTC.Strategy accumulated nearly 80,000 BTC in 2026 alone, including its third-largest single purchase on record of 34,164 BTC.Strategy has risen roughly 250% since early 2024 vs. IBIT's ~55%, driven by its leveraged accumulation structure.Strategy (MSTR), the world's largest publicly traded bitcoin holder, has reclaimed the top spot from BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), surpassing the ETF giant in total BTC holdings for the first time since the second quarter of 2024. The milestone underscores a divergence in strategy between the two vehicles — and signals how aggressively Strategy leaned into bitcoin's bear market to extend its lead.How Strategy pulled aheadStrategy entered 2024 holding 189,150 BTC — a substantial position, but one that was quickly eclipsed as BlackRock's IBIT rapidly scaled following its January 2024 launch. By early Q2 2024, IBIT had accumulated roughly 273,000 BTC compared to Strategy's 214,400, and it maintained that lead consistently throughout the year.The reversal came through aggressive bear market accumulation. As bitcoin fell more than 50% from its October all-time high, Strategy leaned heavily into the drawdown, purchasing nearly 80,000 BTC in 2026 alone. The most recent purchase — 34,164 BTC, Strategy's third-largest acquisition on record — pushed its total holdings to 815,061 BTC, edging past IBIT's 802,824.While the gap of roughly 12,000 BTC is not large in relative terms, it carries significant symbolic weight given IBIT's meteoric rise — the ETF became the fastest in history to reach $70 billion in assets under management and ranks among BlackRock's top revenue-generating products.Two very different vehiclesThe comparison between Strategy and IBIT is compelling precisely because the two operate on fundamentally different models. IBIT is a passive spot ETF designed to track bitcoin's price, offering investors clean, unleveraged exposure to BTC without corporate risk. Strategy, by contrast, is an operating company that uses financial engineering to accumulate bitcoin in a deliberately leveraged manner.MetricStrategy (MSTR)BlackRock IBITStructurePublic companySpot ETFAccumulation methodATM equity, convertible debt, preferred securitiesPassive inflowsLeverageYes — financial engineeringNoBTC holdings815,061 BTC802,824 BTCPerformance since Jan 2024~+250%~+55%Key capital toolSTRC perpetual preferred equityETF inflows STRC: the capital tool that made it possibleA key differentiator in Strategy's recent accumulation push has been its perpetual preferred equity instrument, STRC. Unlike convertible debt or equity issuance — both of which carry dilution or maturity risks — perpetual preferred securities provide a scalable, ongoing source of capital with no fixed repayment obligation. STRC has funded a significant portion of Strategy's 2026 bitcoin purchases, giving the company a structural advantage in sustaining its accumulation pace even during periods of market stress.IBIT's holdings, by comparison, remained relatively stable during the same period, with assets under management seeing only a modest decline — a reflection of its passive nature and dependence on net inflows from external investors rather than active capital deployment.Performance gap tells the real storyThe holdings milestone is striking, but the performance divergence between the two vehicles is arguably more telling. Since IBIT's January 2024 launch, the ETF has gained approximately 55% — a solid return that broadly tracks bitcoin's price appreciation over the period. Strategy, meanwhile, has surged roughly 250% over the same timeframe, a premium driven entirely by its leveraged structure and the market's willingness to assign a bitcoin-acquisition premium to its equity.That premium is a double-edged sword. In a sustained bull market, Strategy's leverage amplifies gains significantly. In a downturn — as seen over the past several months — it creates meaningful downside risk for equity holders that IBIT investors do not face. Strategy's decision to accelerate purchases during bitcoin's 50%-plus decline from its all-time high reflects a conviction-driven bet that current prices represent long-term value, even as the strategy exposes shareholders to continued near-term volatility.For now, Strategy's return to the top of the bitcoin holdings leaderboard marks a notable moment in the ongoing institutionalization of bitcoin — one that highlights the growing diversity of vehicles through which major players are choosing to gain exposure to the asset.

data.title: Russia's Steel Consumption Declines Amid Economic Slowdown
data.description: Russia's largest steel manufacturer, Severstal, reports a further decline in steel consumption during the first quarter due to the economic slowdown. According to Jin10, Severstal's CEO, Alexander Shevelev, stated on Tuesday that the steel industry's situation remains challenging, with Russian steel demand continuing to fall. Consumption in the first quarter of 2026 decreased by 15% compared to the same period last year, surpassing the 14% decline recorded for the entire year of 2025. This downturn in the steel sector mirrors the broader cooling trend in Russia's economy. Last year, Russia experienced its first economic growth slowdown since 2022. The Russian central bank has maintained high interest rates to curb inflation, which has exerted pressure on businesses.

data.title: Japanese Institutional Investors Eye Crypto Expansion Over Next Three Years
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Nomura and Laser Digital reveals that nearly 80% of institutional investors in Japan are planning to increase their cryptocurrency holdings within the next three years. According to NS3.AI, the survey indicates that over half of the respondents are considering allocating between 2% and 5% of their portfolios to digital assets. Additionally, more than 60% of these investors have expressed interest in exploring opportunities in staking, lending, derivatives, and tokenized assets.

data.title: Will Degens Stay as Extreme Volatility Disappears?
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market has been experiencing a period of reduced volatility, raising questions about the future engagement of traders known as 'Degens.' According to PANews, these traders, who thrive on high-risk and high-reward scenarios, may face challenges as the market stabilizes.

The decline in extreme price swings has led to a more predictable trading environment, which could impact the strategies and activities of Degens. This shift in market dynamics is prompting discussions about whether these traders will adapt to the new conditions or seek opportunities elsewhere.

Market analysts suggest that the current trend of diminishing volatility might lead to a decrease in speculative trading activities. As the market becomes less volatile, the appeal of quick profits from rapid price changes diminishes, potentially affecting the participation of traders who rely on such conditions.

The evolving landscape of the cryptocurrency market continues to be a topic of interest, with stakeholders closely monitoring how traders will respond to these changes. The future of Degens in a less volatile market remains uncertain, as they weigh their options in the face of a shifting trading environment.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI's Codex Sees Surge in Weekly Active Users
data.description: OpenAI has reported a significant increase in the weekly active users of its Codex platform, according to The Wall Street Journal. The number of active users has reached 4 million, up from 3 million just two weeks ago. Last month, the platform had over 2 million users. This growth highlights the increasing adoption and interest in AI-driven coding solutions.

data.title: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz remains closed to all traffic and no vessels are permitted to pass.
data.description: According to Iranian sources on the 21st, Iran stated that following the US violation of the ceasefire agreement, access to and from the Strait of Hormuz has been blocked, and all navigation is under strict control by the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy. No vessel is permitted to pass through the strait until a guarantee is received that the naval blockade against Iran will be completely lifted. Furthermore, the passage of any warships is also prohibited. (CCTV News)

data.title: Carmine G. Agnello Sentenced for Misuse of COVID Relief Funds in Crypto Scheme
data.description: Carmine G. Agnello has been sentenced to 15 months in prison for diverting approximately $420,000 of fraudulently obtained COVID relief funds into a cryptocurrency business. According to NS3.AI, the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Eastern District of New York revealed that Agnello secured around $1.1 million through false applications for SBA Economic Injury Disaster Loans linked to Crown Auto Parts & Recycling. In addition to the prison term, the court mandated Agnello to pay $1,268,302 in restitution, serve two years of supervised release, and complete 100 hours of community service.

data.title: Elon Musk Supports Telegram Founder in Criticism of EU and UK Content Moderation
data.description: Telegram founder Pavel Durov has criticized EU and UK authorities, accusing them of leveraging child safety concerns to negotiate private content moderation agreements with social media executives. According to NS3.AI, Elon Musk has expressed support for Durov's stance. On the same day, French prosecutors requested Musk's presence for a voluntary interview regarding allegations that X, his platform, enabled the distribution of child abuse material and deepfakes. Durov also mentioned that his own legal issues in France are still under investigation.

data.title: Texas Oil Pipelines Strain Under High Demand Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Texas oil pipelines are operating beyond their intended capacity due to a surge in international demand for U.S. crude. Bloomberg posted on X that this increased demand is a result of disruptions in oil shipments caused by the ongoing conflict involving Iran. The situation has led to a significant strain on the infrastructure, as global markets seek alternatives to the stranded cargoes. The heightened activity underscores the critical role of U.S. oil in stabilizing global energy supplies amid geopolitical tensions.
